response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, some Java/C++), but the role demands deep expertise in Java, Kotlin, JVM internals, and the Spring ecosystem — none of which are evident in the resume. Java appears only as a listed skill with limited applied context (robotic arms, mobile orchestration years ago), Kotlin and Spring are absent entirely, and there is no IDE/language-tooling background beyond brief C/C++ work on LabWindows/CVI a decade ago. The genuine interest in IDE internals required by the role is not demonstrated.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale systems across web, backend, and embedded
Prior IDE-adjacent experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
Kotlin not mentioned; Java exposure is shallow and dated
No evidence of JVM internals knowledge or modern IDE/language-tooling work

key_factors.factor: Proven senior engineer on complex systems
